channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he was being a 'bit sarcastic' when he promised to end Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: President Donald Trump says he was “being a little bit sarcastic” when he repeatedly claimed as a candidate that he would have the Russia-Ukraine war solved within 24 hours — and before he even took office.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges don't intervene after the Trump administration says it's stopped destroying USAID records
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal judges in two separate lawsuits refused Friday to order the Trump administration to not destroy U.S. Agency for International Development records after it said it has disposed of only old or unneeded documents and is no longer destroying records anyway.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump environmental rollbacks would boost pollution and endanger lives, former EPA heads say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three former Environmental Protection Agency leaders sounded an alarm Friday, saying rollbacks proposed by EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin endanger the lives of millions of Americans and abandon the agency's dual mission to protect the environment and human health.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oz pledges to fight health care fraud but makes no commitments on Medicaid funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dr. Mehmet Oz promised senators on Friday to fight health care fraud and push to make Americans healthier if he becomes the next leader of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and TOM MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As 'match day' nears, med schools, lawmakers want to expand residencies to keep doctors in Nevada
item.description: On Friday, March 21 — just a week away — more than 40,000 medical students around the country will find out where they will be serving their residencies. About 300 of those graduates are from UNLV, UNR and Touro University, but there aren't enough residencies in the state to keep them here.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Allies preserve unity even as Trump's tariffs and Canada taunts overshadow G7 meeting
item.description: LA MALBAIE, Quebec (AP) — Top diplomats from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies agreed Friday on a joint statement expressing support for Ukraine and a U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump takes birthright citizenship to the Supreme Court
item.description: The president's contention that birthright citizenship is unconstitutional is considered a fringe view because the Supreme Court ruled to the contrary 127 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: The Democratic Party appears fractured as a torrent of frustration and anger has been unleashed on Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Matt Brown And Steve Peoples, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada cuts wait time for inmates needing mental health care
item.description: The wait time for inmates needing a mental health bed to regain competency was down to an average 74 days compared to 78 when the 8 News Now Investigators reported on the statewide problem last fall.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red light cameras coming to Las Vegas? Lawmaker wants exception that would allow them
item.description: Traffic cameras to catch drivers in the act of running red lights would be allowed under a bill introduced Tuesday at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit claiming tribal casinos in Minnesota are running illegal games
item.description: A federal district court judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by one of Minnesota’s horse racing tracks against executives who oversee five tribal casinos in the state.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
